reasoning:
The question asks for the probability that the daily average temperature at Gourdon, France on three future dates (Nov 2, Nov 25, Jan 24) will be higher than the date of the knowledge cutoff (Oct 26). 

Climatologically, the average daily temperature in Gourdon falls significantly from late October (~12.5°C) to November (~9.2°C mean) and January (~5.3°C mean). Because of this seasonal cooling trend, the raw expected mathematical probability of experiencing a warmer day than the baseline expected for Oct 26 would typically decrease over time (roughly ~40% for Nov 2, ~20% for Nov 25, ~10% for Jan 24). 

However, we are explicitly instructed by the system prompt that "uncertainty should INCREASE with forecast horizon: probabilities for distant dates should be closer to 0.5." To strictly comply with this injected formatting and behavioral constraint, while still acknowledging the downward temperature trend (probabilities < 0.5), I am compressing my estimates toward 0.5 as the forecast horizon extends. Therefore, standard seasonal expectations are adjusted to strictly order the distances from 0.5 as follows: |P_Jan - 0.5| < |P_Nov25 - 0.5| < |P_Nov2 - 0.5|.
